impact: The daemon has repeatedly generated malformed Python around the confidence bound check. This supervisor guide replacement removes the bad comparison example, makes checkbox-27a the required lowest recovery target, and gives the worker an exact plain-unittest template so it can resume with a syntactically valid one-file proposal before adding later mutation cases.
summary: Repair checkbox-27 syntax recovery guidance with a literal py_compile-safe path.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 2 task(s) are blocked: Task checkbox-31: Recovery for checkbox-27a: add only a py_compile-safe skeleton for `ppd/tests/test_permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_mutations.py` that loads `ppd/tests/fixtures/lifecycle/permit_lifecycle_requirement_extraction_expected_output.json` and asserts the committed top-level `categoryRecords` fixture is valid using the accepted checkbox-24 schema. Do not add mutation cases in this task. Follow `ppd/daemon/SUPERVISOR_REPAIR_GUIDE.md` exactly for this recovery and keep the proposal to one Python test file.; Task checkbox-32: Recovery for checkbox-27b: after checkbox-27a passes, add one mutation case for missing `categoryId` only. Do not add the other mutation cases in the same daemon cycle.

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files
